spec_match.rationale: The demo is highly compliant, demonstrating integrity checks in font declarations and background images with appropriate CORS configurations.

interactive.rationale: The 'Compute a real hash' section uses actual browser crypto APIs to fetch a URL and generate real SHA-256 integrity values, making it highly interactive and functional.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned with the parent index's description of demonstrating hash verification, cascade failure, and hash mismatches.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers hash format regex validation and fetch/CORS failure handling within the SHA-256 calculator.

references.rationale: Includes direct links to other modifiers and the parent index, though it should include a direct link to ChromeStatus on-page.

design_system.rationale: Flawless implementation of design rules, including consistent list items, borders, colors, and layout spacing.

openQuestions.title: Make the font load test genuinely live using the FontFace API
openQuestions.detail: The 'Test Font Load' button currently only checks the general support flag and shows static descriptive texts rather than triggering a real load. We can make this completely live by injecting a dynamic @font-face style with correct vs wrong hashes, then utilizing document.fonts.load() to verify if the browser successfully loads the custom font or rejects it in practice, showing a real-time visual test result.

openQuestions.title: Add direct ChromeStatus and SRI spec reference links
openQuestions.detail: Adding a direct references section with links to ChromeStatus and the W3C Subresource Integrity spec on this page will ensure compliance and improve navigation for developers studying the spec contracts.

summary: An outstanding educational demo that features a fully functional SHA-256 integrity hash calculator using real browser crypto APIs. It is beautifully styled and highly interactive, with minor room for improvement in turning the simulated font-loading test into a live FontFace API test.
